topic: hijab styling for a client presentation

turns.content: okay so I have a big client presentation on Thursday and I want to look proper put together. I wear hijab and I always stress about styling it for these things. like I want to look polished and professional but also ME, you know?

Totally valid — presentations are one of those times where you want everything to feel intentional, and hijab styling is part of that overall look. What kind of setting is this? Big boardroom, smaller meeting room, virtual? And what's your usual go-to style — do you normally do a wrap, a turban style, or something else?

its in person, proper boardroom. big client, like 12 people from their side. I usually do a simple wrap for work — chiffon, pinned under the chin. it looks fine day to day but for something like this I want it to look more elevated if that makes sense

Makes complete sense — the chin-pin chiffon wrap is a great everyday style but it can look a bit casual in a boardroom setting where everyone else is in sharp blazers. A few ideas to elevate it without going too far from your comfort zone:

1. **Structured wrap with a side drape** — same chiffon fabric but instead of pinning under the chin, you bring one side across and drape it over the opposite shoulder. Looks more polished and frames your face really well. Works great with a blazer because the drape sits on top of the shoulder.

2. **Switch the fabric** — keep the same wrap style you're comfortable with but use a slightly heavier fabric like jersey or a premium modal. It holds its shape better and doesn't shift around during a presentation, which means less fidgeting.

3. **Turban-style wrap** — more modern, very clean lines. Though if you don't normally wear this style it might feel unfamiliar on a high-pressure day.

Which of those sounds most like you?

the side drape thing sounds nice tbh. I don't want to do turban cos I've never really worn that style and I'd just be thinking about it the whole time. what colour would you go with? I'm wearing a navy blazer and white blouse

Navy blazer and white blouse is a great foundation — very clean and authoritative. For the hijab colour, you've got a few solid options:

- **Nude/taupe** — keeps everything tonal and polished. Doesn't compete with the blazer, lets your face be the focus. Very boardroom-appropriate.
- **Soft grey** — complements navy beautifully without being boring. Adds dimension.
- **Deep burgundy or emerald** — if you want a subtle pop of colour. Still professional but shows personality. Burgundy with navy is a really sophisticated combination.

I'd probably lean nude/taupe or burgundy depending on how bold you're feeling. What's your skin tone like? That can help narrow it down.
I'm medium-dark, warm toned. I think burgundy could be nice actually. I have a burgundy chiffon that I wear to like family events but never thought to wear it to work

Burgundy with warm medium-dark skin is a beautiful combination — it'll look rich without washing you out. And that colour against navy is genuinely striking in a subtle way. Very corporate but with character.

Since it's chiffon, one tip: use an underscarf or a bonnet cap underneath. It gives the wrap more structure, prevents slipping during the presentation, and you won't have to adjust it when you're trying to look confident and focused. If you have a black or nude bonnet that works best.

Also — if you're standing at the front presenting, make sure the drape is pinned securely enough that it doesn't shift when you turn your head or gesture. A couple of small magnetic hijab pins instead of regular safety pins can help. They're more secure and you don't get the visible pin look.
wait magnetic pins?? I've been using safety pins this whole time like an amateur loool. where do I get those

Loool you'd be surprised how many people are still on safety pins — magnetic pins are a game changer though. They hold without poking through the fabric so you get a cleaner finish and no snag marks on your nice scarves.

You can get them from:
- **Amazon** — search "magnetic hijab pins," lots of options. The round flat ones are best for professional settings, they're almost invisible.
- **Haute Hijab** — if you want quality ones that actually last. Their magnetic pins are strong.
- **Modanisa** — big online hijab retailer, good range.
- Or honestly any hijab shop on the high street if you have one nearby.

They come in different strengths — for chiffon you want medium strength. The super strong ones are better for thicker fabrics. Get a set and you'll never go back to safety pins.
ordering the Amazon ones rn. okay so game plan: burgundy chiffon, side drape, bonnet cap underneath, magnetic pins. navy blazer white blouse. what about jewellery? I usually wear small gold studs

Small gold studs are perfect — don't change those. They're visible, elegant, and they pick up the warmth in your skin tone and the burgundy. You don't need statement earrings when your hijab is already framing your face nicely.

If you want to add one thing: a simple gold watch or a thin gold bracelet. It catches the eye when you're gesturing during the presentation, which is a subtle authority cue. But honestly the studs alone are more than enough.

Avoid necklaces since they'll be under the scarf anyway, and skip any noisy bangles — you don't want clicking sounds when you're pointing at slides.

this is bare helpful actually thank you. I always overthink this stuff before big meetings. half the time I end up just doing my normal thing and then feeling underdressed. this time I'm going to actually plan it out

Planning it out the night before makes a massive difference — you walk in feeling like you chose the look rather than defaulting to it, and that confidence genuinely shows in how you present. Try the full look on Wednesday night so you can troubleshoot the drape and pin placement without rushing Thursday morning.

You've got a strong outfit plan. Go smash that presentation.
wallah I'm actually feeling good about it now. thanks for this, genuinely. right I need to go practice my slides loool bye
Go nail those slides! Good luck Thursday — you've got this.
